food_name: Pinot Nero Superiore DOC, Cantina Breganze, Veneto, Italy (2016)
aliases: Pinot Nero Superiore DOC Savardo Cantina Breganze 2016

english: Pinot Nero Superiore DOC Savardo Cantina Breganze 2016

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a general 'red wine' class (FOODON_03301636). No specific entry exists for Pinot Nero Superiore DOC Breganze; closest_parent used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code GBA4S covers red wine generically. No specific DOC Pinot Nero Breganze entry exists in FoodEx2; closest_parent used.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 35748005 is the closest defensible parent for wine as a substance. No Pinot Nero-specific code exists.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of red wine is approximately 150 ml (roughly 150 g), equivalent to one glass as typically poured in a restaurant setting.

nutrient_source.title: Wine, table, red — USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy (FDC ID 174835)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: USDA SR Legacy entry for generic table red wine used as the closest approved source. Pinot Nero Superiore DOC Savardo is a dry, full-bodied Italian red wine; its macronutrient profile is consistent with generic dry red wine values. No HPB, ICMR-NIN, or FAO/INFOODS entry exists for this specific Italian DOC wine. USDA FDC SR Legacy is the highest-priority approved source available for this Western alcoholic beverage.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of alcoholic beverages — University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Buyken et al.; Brand-Miller et al.)
gi_source.source_match_notes: Dry red wine contains minimal fermentable carbohydrates (~2.6 g/100 ml, predominantly residual sugars). The University of Sydney GI Database and peer-reviewed literature (Brand-Miller et al.) consistently classify dry wine as having a negligible glycemic response, effectively GI < 15. Per the Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule, glycemic_index is set to 1 (the lowest valid positive sentinel) to indicate an effectively zero glycemic response. gi_category is 'low' (GI ≤ 55). glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 given the negligible carbohydrate content per serving.

hypertension.guidance: Regular alcohol consumption, even at moderate levels, is associated with elevated blood pressure; individuals with hypertension should limit red wine intake to no more than one standard drink per day and consult their physician, as the low sodium content (4 mg/100 ml) does not offset the pressor effect of ethanol.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Alcohol-induced hypoglycaemia risk and caloric contribution
type2_diabetes.guidance: Dry red wine has a negligible glycemic index due to very low residual sugar (~0.62 g/100 ml), but ethanol can mask hypoglycaemia symptoms and interfere with gluconeogenesis; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should consume no more than one standard glass with a meal and monitor blood glucose closely.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Ethanol content, polyphenols, and caloric density
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: While moderate red wine consumption has been associated with polyphenol-mediated cardioprotective effects in observational studies, current clinical guidelines do not recommend initiating alcohol consumption for cardiovascular benefit; individuals with established cardiovascular disease should limit intake to ≤1 standard drink per day and prioritise lifestyle modifications.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Pinot Nero Superiore DOC Savardo is a premium Italian red wine produced by Cantina Breganze, a cooperative winery located in the Breganze DOC zone of the Veneto region in northeastern Italy. The 2016 vintage is made from Pinot Nero grapes (the Italian name for Pinot Noir), a thin-skinned, early-ripening variety known for producing elegant, medium-bodied red wines. The wine presents a medium-deep ruby red colour with a refined, fruity aromatic profile featuring hints of red berries, cherry, and subtle spice. On the palate it is balanced, dry, and full-bodied with good tannic structure and a clean finish. Nutritionally, dry red wine provides approximately 85 kcal per 100 ml, derived primarily from ethanol (~11–13% ABV) rather than carbohydrates; residual sugar is very low (~0.62 g/100 ml), making it a low-glycemic beverage. Fat content is effectively zero (naturally zero — wine contains no dietary fat). Protein is negligible (0.07 g/100 ml). Notable micronutrients include potassium (127 mg/100 ml), iron (0.46 mg/100 ml), and polyphenolic compounds including resveratrol and anthocyanins, which have been studied for antioxidant properties. Clinically, the primary concern for all patient groups is ethanol content: alcohol can raise blood pressure, interfere with glycaemic control in Type 2 Diabetes, and interact with cardiovascular medications. Sulphites are present as a preservative and represent the main allergen. This wine is served at Ricciotti - The Riverwalk restaurant in Singapore and is listed on field survey data at SGD 86.39 per bottle.

meal_context: wine with pasta or red meat

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy generic dry red wine (FDC ID 174835), which is the closest approved source available. No HPB, ICMR-NIN, FAO/INFOODS, or other primary regional source covers this specific Italian DOC wine. The macronutrient profile of Pinot Nero Superiore DOC Savardo is consistent with generic dry red wine; however, exact values for this specific vintage and producer are not available in any approved database, warranting medium rather than high confidence.
notes: 1. fat_g is 0.0: naturally zero — wine contains no dietary fat by nature. saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are all 0.0: naturally zero for the same reason. dietary_fibre_g is 0.0: naturally zero — fermented grape juice contains no dietary fibre. cholesterol_mg is 0.0: naturally zero — plant-derived fermented beverage. vitamin_c_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FDC SR Legacy (wine processing destroys ascorbic acid). vitamin_a_mcg_rae is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FDC SR Legacy. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero — no animal-derived ingredients. vitamin_d_mcg is 0.0: naturally zero — no UV-exposed animal products. vitamin_e_mg is 0.0: confirmed zero per USDA FDC SR Legacy for red wine. 2. glycemic_index is set to 1 (effectively zero) as this beverage contains negligible glycemic carbohydrates; gi_category is low per convention and per the Veritas zero-calorie beverage glycemic rule. glycemic_load_per_serving is 0.0 given the negligible carbohydrate content per 150 g serving (GL = GI/100 × carb_g_per_serving = 1/100 × 3.9 ≈ 0.04, rounded to 0.0). 3. Input food_group was 'Grains and staples' which is incorrect for a wine; corrected to 'Beverages'. 4. The input cuisine_tags (Pasta, Pizza, Chicken Dish) reflect the restaurant context (Ricciotti - The Riverwalk) rather than the wine itself and are not carried forward as food classification attributes. 5. Ontology codes use closest_parent match type throughout as no specific DOC Pinot Nero Breganze entry exists in FoodOn, FoodEx2, or SNOMED CT. 6. is_vegan set to false as many wines use animal-derived fining agents (egg whites, isinglass, casein); Cantina Breganze vegan status is not confirmed. 7. All -1.0 sentinel values have been replaced with actual USDA FDC values where available; no -1 sentinels remain in this record.
